title: Missing women on Indian streets
summary:
How absent are women from city streets in the developing world? We answer this question using GPS-linked wearable cameras and randomized street audits across ~900 kilometers of roads in greater Mumbai. Across 4000+ street images containing 23,000+ visible person observations, women account for 16.4% of visible people in Mumbai and 14.7% in Navi Mumbai, far […]

title: Can AI models consent to their own constitutions?
summary:
From Nick Caputo: NEW paper from me on SSRN: Can Claude consent to its own Constitution? AI constitutions (like Claude’s Constitution and the OpenAI Model Spec) are real constitutions, and we need to take how they govern us – and the AIs they create – seriously. In this paper, I apply constitutional theory’s oldest paradox […]

title: Mainland China markets follow US in embracing tech stock dominance
summary:
Mainland China’s stock markets are converging with those in the US when it comes to the concentration of technology companies, with the sector becoming the biggest constituent of key equity benchmarks.

For decades, warnings of Hong Kong’s terminal decline have arrived in periodic waves.

title: China extends gold-buying binge to 20th month amid Beijing’s de-dollarisation push

China’s central bank extended its gold-buying streak to a 20th straight month in June, adding 480,000 troy ounces (14.93 tonnes) to its reserves as policymakers continued to accumulate bullion amid de-dollarisation discussions.
The People’s Bank of China said gold holdings rose to 75.44 million troy ounces (2,346 tonnes) at the end of June. The latest addition extended the longest buying streak since 2015.
